2606.31635.title: A Tutorial on Autonomous Fault-Tolerant Control Using Knowledge-Grounded LLM Agents
2606.31635.paper_summary: Fault recovery in process plants still relies heavily on plant operators, especially when faults fall outside predefined supervisory logic. Operators interpret alarms, procedures, P\&IDs, interlocks, and process trends, then decide how to move the plant to a safe operating mode without triggering a shutdown. This paper examines how Large Language Model (LLM) agents can support such recovery decisions. The proposed framework treats the LLM as a constrained supervisory planner. It uses plant-specific knowledge to propose recovery actions, and every proposal is checked by an external validator (symbolic or simulation-based) before actuation. The paper develops three design dimensions for applying the framework: the recovery patterns for which LLM agents are useful, the validation strategies that separate admissible from inadmissible proposals, and the deployment constraints imposed by latency, knowledge engineering, safety integration, and model lifecycle management. To make the framework directly usable, two openly available executable Python environments are provided. Both re-implement established case studies, a modular mixing module and a continuous stirred-tank reactor, extended with configurable faults and defined interfaces for custom recovery and validation methods.
2606.31635.paper_summary_zh: 故障恢復在流程工廠中仍然高度依賴工廠操作員，特別是當故障超出預定的監控邏輯時。操作員解釋警報、程序、P&ID、聯鎖和過程趨勢，然後決定如何將工廠移至安全操作模式而不觸發停機。本文探討大型語言模型（LLM）代理如何支持這種恢復決策。所提出的框架將LLM視為受限的監控規劃者。它利用特定於工廠的知識來提出恢復行動，並在執行之前由外部驗證者（符號或基於模擬）檢查每個提議。本文為應用該框架開發了三個設計維度：LLM代理有用的恢復模式、將可接受提議與不可接受提議區分開來的驗證策略，以及由延遲、知識工程、安全整合和模型生命週期管理施加的部署限制。為了使該框架可以直接使用，提供了兩個公開可用的可執行Python環境。這兩者都重新實現了已建立的案例研究，一個模塊化混合模塊和一個連續搖動槽反應器，並擴展了可配置的故障和自定義恢復及驗證方法的定義接口。
2606.31635.author: Javal Vyas et.al.
2606.31635.authors: Javal Vyas, Milapji Singh Gill, Artan Markaj, Felix Gehlhoff, Mehmet Mercangöz
